# terrible_kubernetes_core_k8s_log (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `all_containers` (Boolean) If set to `true`, retrieve all containers' logs in the pod(s). mutually exclusive with `container`.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `container` (String) Use to specify the container within a pod to grab the log from. If there is only one container, this will default to that container. If there is more than one container, this option is required or set *all_containers* to `true`. mutually exclusive with `all_containers`.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `kind` (String) Use to specify an object model. Use in conjunction with *api_version*, *name*, and *namespace* to identify a specific object. If using *label_selectors*, cannot be overridden.
- `label_selectors` (String) List of label selectors to use to filter results Only one of *name* or *label_selectors* may be provided.
- `name` (String) Use to specify an object name. Use in conjunction with *api_version*, *kind* and *namespace* to identify a specific object. Only one of *name* or *label_selectors* may be provided.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `previous` (Boolean) If `true`, print the logs for the previous instance of the container in a pod if it exists.
- `since_seconds` (String) A relative time in seconds before the current time from which to show logs.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tail_lines` (Number) A number of lines from the end of the logs to retrieve.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `log` (String) The text log of the object
- `log_lines` (String) The log of the object, split on newlines
